```
42	
43	- `bridge_task_subscriptions` as the bridge-owned target table.
44	- Cursor identity `consumer_id = "bridge_task_subscription:<subscription_id>"`, `stream_name = "task_events"`, and `subject_id = <task_id>`.
45	- A durable replay flow that reads `task_events` by `event_seq`, filters terminal task events, reloads current task state, delivers through `bridges/deliver`, and advances the cursor only after confirmed success.
46	- The rule that hook or `EventObserver` wake-up is only a nudge; replay authority remains durable `task_events.event_seq`.
47	- The rule that this notifier is one-shot terminal delivery and must not use prompt/session `DeliveryBroker` as its primary consumer path.
48	- The terminal notification envelope with deterministic `delivery_id`, `event_type = "final"`, `final = true`, and numeric `seq = task_events.event_seq`.
49	- MVP API surface: `POST /api/tasks/:id/notifications/bridges`, `GET /api/tasks/:id/notifications/bridges`, and `DELETE /api/tasks/:id/notifications/bridges/:subscription_id`.
50	
51	### B-005 - Spawn failure observer/reset call sites
52	
53	Specified that `internal/session.Manager` classifies task-session spawn failures, calls `task.Service.IncrementSpawnFailure` after classification, and resets counters through `AttachRunSession` only after successful task session attach. Claim alone no longer resets the breaker.
54	
55	### B-006 - Public `MaintainCurrentRunID` misuse surface
56	
57	Removed the public projection-maintenance interface from the TechSpec. The projection helper shape is documented only as an internal unexported helper inside `internal/task`, and boundary tests must fail if scheduler, coordinator, API, bridge, notification, extension, or web-facing code imports or calls it directly.
58	
59	### B-007 - Task-level endpoint authorization
60	
61	Added explicit operator-only authorization language and transport parity expectations for task-level complete/fail endpoints. Session actors must use `/api/agent/tasks/:run_id/complete|fail`, which resolves the active lease through session-bound lookup.
62	
63	## Incorporated Nits
64	
65	### N-001 - `OrchestrationManager` is logical decomposition
66	
67	Clarified that `OrchestrationManager` is a logical decomposition of the existing `task.Manager` / `task.Service` implementation, not a second service struct, second queue authority, or second daemon composition-root dependency.
68	
69	### N-002 - Idempotent replay needs `last_delivery_id`
70	
71	Added `last_delivery_id` to `notification_cursors`, `Cursor`, and observability fields. `Advance` may accept idempotent replay only when both `last_sequence` and `delivery_id` match the last confirmed cursor state.
72	
73	### N-003 - Scheduler events are observe events only
74	
75	Clarified that `scheduler.*` signals are `internal/observe` typed events and this TechSpec does not introduce a scheduler hook taxonomy.
76	
77	### N-004 - HTTP/UDS parity matrix
78	
79	Added an explicit HTTP/UDS parity matrix for task-level terminal endpoints, bridge notification subscription endpoints, run-level terminal endpoints, agent terminal endpoints, agent context, and dashboard health.
80	
81	### N-006 - Loader-side `requires_active_task_claim`
82	
83	Added an implementation dependency requiring bundled skill loader support for `metadata.agh.always_load.requires_active_task_claim` if it does not already exist.
84	
85	### N-007 - Max watchdog duration
86	
87	Pinned the maximum configured runtime watchdog duration to `24h` and required config validation to reject larger values.
88	
89	### N-008 - Full max-runtime managed-stop/race test
90	
91	Added integration coverage for the sequence: request stop, grace expiry, force stop, terminal write, projection clear, plus a race where the worker sends `CompleteRunLease` after stop request but before terminal write.
92	
93	### N-009 - `Last-Event-ID` precedence
94	
95	Specified that `Last-Event-ID` takes precedence over `?after_sequence` on SSE reconnect, while first-open uses `?after_sequence` seeded from `latest_event_seq`.
96	
97	### N-010 - Delete/replace targets
98	
99	Expanded delete/replace targets for active task-level force-close behavior, exported current-run projection maintenance APIs, bridge-specific cursor side-state, and terminal task notification paths that try to use prompt/session `DeliveryBroker`.
100	
101	## Deferred
102	
103	- `N-005` remains optional/deferred. The TechSpec keeps example CLI task-level terminal names, but no extra mandatory rename work was added for this nit.
104	
105	## Files Updated
106	
107	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/_techspec.md`
108	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-001-orchestration-hardening-extends-existing-autonomy.md`
109	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-002-queryable-orchestration-state.md`
110	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-003-shared-durable-notification-cursors.md`
111	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-004-minimal-task-orchestration-config.md`
112	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-005-current-run-id-denormalized-projection.md`
113	- `.compozy/tasks/orch-improvs/adrs/adr-006-bundled-orchestration-skills-are-instructional.md`
114	
115	## Validation
116	
117	- Verified TechSpec and ADR fenced code blocks are balanced.
118	- Verified round 2 markers are present for active-run rejection, synthetic terminal concurrency, spawn-circuit claim filtering, bridge terminal notifications, `last_delivery_id`, HTTP/UDS parity matrix, scheduler observe-only events, `requires_active_task_claim`, `24h` watchdog validation, `Last-Event-ID` precedence, and delete/replace targets.
119	- Did not run `make verify`; this is still a spec-review workflow decision point, not an implementation completion point.
120	
```

ake mechanism, ambiguous review-request transaction boundary, undef
ined `RecordRunReview` continuation-enqueue atomicity, and unstated `ParticipantPolicy` enforcement surface — that pr
